rulings.Case Title: EICON INC VS WORLDPOINT INTERACTIVE INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Quitable Setoff And Declaration Of The Parties' Rights And Duties With Respect To The January 23, 2002, Default Judgment Set Aside As Void On Its Face
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2009, line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT MASSIMO FUCHS Motion For Equitable Setoff and Declaration of the Parties' Rights and Duties with Respect To The January 23, 2002, Default Judgment Set Aside As Void On Its Face. Motion is denied. Cross-defendant does not sufficiently establish that he owned the property, or had an enforceable lien on the property. Request for sanctions is denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCESS NORMAN VS. KRISTINA ZEBRATSKI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT KRISTINA ZEBRATSKI, GEORGE CRAIG, and, CRAWLEY WARREN INSURANCE SERVICES, INC., DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer is overruled as to first cause of action for violation of FEHA. Pleading sufficient to apprise defendant that plaintiff is alleging race discrimination. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to third, cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. The cause of action arises out of conduct for which worker's compensation is exclusive remedy, and supervisors not individually liable. See Reno vs. Baird (1998) 18 Cal. 4th 640. Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the sixth (sic) cause of action for Intentional infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff must allege which defendants are the subject of the cause of action (CRC 2.112.) Plaintiff must plead extreme and outrageous conduct by a defendant, the intention to cause or reckless disregard of the probability of causing emotional distress, severe emotional suffering, and actual and proximate causation of the emotional distress. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAVID D BATISTE SR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Of Petition For Court To Accept Petitioners Filing Of Claim #09-02772 For Not Being A Duplicate Filing. Court Order Relieving Petitioner From The Provisions Of Goverment Code 945.4
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 29, 2009, line 5, PETITIONER DAVID BATISTE, and, ROSE SHEPPARD Motion Of Petition For Court To Accept Petitioners Filing Of Claim #09-02772 For Not Being A Duplicate Filing. Court Order Relieving Petitioner From The Provisions Of Goverment Code 945.4. Petition is denied. Claim is time barred. Claim relating to damage to real property must be submitted no later than one year after accrual. Government Code section 911.2(a). Damages occurred in 2002 and 2005. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. No express or implied assurance that defendant would cover plaintiffs' damage made such that defendant estopped from bringing a statute of limitations defense. =(302/CWW)
